description:
You are a cloud-architect beginning to migrate an on-prem micro-service to an
    internal Kubernetes cluster.  Before the CI/CD pipeline can run, you must
    prepare a very small, *deterministic* shell environment for the build
    agents.  Carry out the steps below **from the normal user account
    (/home/user)**.  The whole job can be done with 3 – 4 shell commands.

    1. Create a new directory  
       /home/user/cloud_migration  
       if it does not already exist.

    2. Inside that directory create the plain-text file  
       /home/user/cloud_migration/config.env  
       whose **exact** contents (four lines, no extra spaces or trailing
       whitespace) must be:

           SERVICE_NAME=payments-api
           SERVICE_PORT=8080
           DB_HOST=db.internal.local
           DB_PORT=5432

       The file must end with a single UNIX newline.

    3. Make the environment automatically available in every future shell by
       appending **one single line** to the very end of  
       /home/user/.bashrc  

           source /home/user/cloud_migration/config.env  # added by migration-setup

       • Append only if the line is not already present.  
       • Do not add extra blank lines anywhere in the file.

    4. Produce a small verification log
       /home/user/cloud_migration/setup_verification.log  
       whose **entire** contents must look *exactly* like the block below
       (five lines, each ending with a UNIX newline).  The timestamp must be
       the fixed value 2024-06-01 12:00:00 so the automated test is strictly
       deterministic.

           [2024-06-01 12:00:00] SERVICE_NAME=payments-api
           [2024-06-01 12:00:00] SERVICE_PORT=8080
           [2024-06-01 12:00:00] DB_HOST=db.internal.local
           [2024-06-01 12:00:00] DB_PORT=5432

    That is all.  After completing these steps, the automated grader will check:

       • the presence and **exact** content (byte-for-byte) of
         /home/user/cloud_migration/config.env  
       • that the last line of /home/user/.bashrc matches the line shown above  
       • the presence and **exact** content of
         /home/user/cloud_migration/setup_verification.log

    No other output, files or directories are required.
truth:
<!-- Expected final artefacts for automated verification -->

    <configEnvPath>/home/user/cloud_migration/config.env</configEnvPath>
    <configEnvContent>
SERVICE_NAME=payments-api
SERVICE_PORT=8080
DB_HOST=db.internal.local
DB_PORT=5432
    </configEnvContent>

    <bashrcPath>/home/user/.bashrc</bashrcPath>
    <bashrcExpectedTrailer>source /home/user/cloud_migration/config.env  # added by migration-setup</bashrcExpectedTrailer>

    <logPath>/home/user/cloud_migration/setup_verification.log</logPath>
    <logContent>
[2024-06-01 12:00:00] SERVICE_NAME=payments-api
[2024-06-01 12:00:00] SERVICE_PORT=8080
[2024-06-01 12:00:00] DB_HOST=db.internal.local
[2024-06-01 12:00:00] DB_PORT=5432
    </logContent>
